Consultation.title: Consultation
Consultation.description: Represents a user inquiry for a viability study. All writes are now handled by a secure server-side API route.

cedula.description: National identification number (Cedula) of the person making the inquiry.

nombre.description: Full name of the person making the inquiry.

contacto.description: WhatsApp contact number provided by the user.

aceptoTerminos.description: Indicates whether the user has accepted the terms and conditions.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the consultation record was created.

notificationStatus.description: Status of the Telegram notification, managed server-side.

notifiedAt.description: Timestamp when the Telegram notification was successfully sent.

ShowcaseImages.title: Showcase Images
ShowcaseImages.description: Stores URLs for the 'before' and 'after' images on the homepage.

Case.title: Case
Case.description: Represents an administrative debt removal process for a client, typically initiated after a Consultation is deemed viable.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Case entity.

consultationId.description: Reference to the Consultation that initiated this case. (Relationship: Consultation 1:1 Case)

caseReferenceNumber.description: Internal or external unique reference number assigned to this administrative case.

currentStatus.description: Current progress status of the administrative case (e.g., 'pending_documents', 'submitted_to_transit', 'approved', 'rejected', 'closed').

initialDebtAmount.description: Total debt amount identified before the administrative process began.

finalOutcomeDescription.description: A summary description of the final result of the case (e.g., 'Debt fully prescribed', 'Partial debt reduction').

caseStartedAt.description: Timestamp when the administrative case officially began.

caseCompletedAt.description: Timestamp when the administrative case was officially completed or closed.

beforeSimitImageId.description: Reference to the Asset entity representing the 'before' SIMIT screenshot. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Case)

afterSimitImageId.description: Reference to the Asset entity representing the 'after' SIMIT screenshot. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Case)

LegalDocumentSummary.title: LegalDocumentSummary
LegalDocumentSummary.description: Stores AI-generated summaries and simplified explanations of legal documents pertinent to a specific case.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalDocumentSummary entity.

caseId.description: Reference to the Case this summary belongs to. (Relationship: Case 1:N LegalDocumentSummary)

documentTitle.description: Title or brief description of the original legal document being summarized.

summaryContent.description: The AI-generated detailed summary of the legal document.

keyLegalPoints.description: An array of key legal principles or laws identified and incorporated in the summary.

simplifiedExplanation.description: A simplified, user-friendly explanation derived from the summary content.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this summary was generated.

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Manages metadata for digital assets (e.g., images, PDFs) stored in an external CDN like Cloudinary or Vercel Blob, related to cases or consultations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

url.description: The public URL where the asset is hosted (e.g., Cloudinary, Vercel Blob).

fileName.description: Original filename of the uploaded asset.

description.description: Descriptive text for the asset, often used for accessibility (alt text).

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the asset was uploaded and this record was created.

caseId.description: Reference to the Case this asset is associated with. (Relationship: Case 1:N Asset)

definition.description: Stores UIDs of users with admin privileges. The mere existence of a document for a given UID grants administrative access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Singleton document storing homepage showcase images. Publicly readable, admin-writable.
Collection for user inquiries. All client-side writes are now denied. Creation is handled by a secure server-side API route. Management (read/update/delete) is restricted to admins.

params.name: consultationId
params.description: Unique identifier for a consultation document.

definition.description: Collection representing an administrative debt removal process for a client. Each case is managed by an administrator, whose Firebase UID is stored in the 'assignedToId' field. Includes 'createdAt' and 'updatedAt' for auditing purposes.

params.name: caseId
params.description: Unique identifier for a case document.

definition.description: Subcollection storing AI-generated summaries of legal documents pertinent to a specific case. Includes denormalized 'caseAssignedToId' for authorization independence, ensuring access is tied to the parent case's assigned administrator without requiring cross-document reads.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent case.

params.name: summaryId
params.description: Unique identifier for a legal document summary.

definition.description: Subcollection managing metadata for digital assets (e.g., images, PDFs) associated with a specific case, stored in an external CDN. Includes denormalized 'caseAssignedToId' for authorization independence, ensuring access is tied to the parent case's assigned administrator without requiring cross-document reads.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent case.

params.name: assetId
params.description: Unique identifier for an asset metadata document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for VialClear prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation principles. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design ensures authorization independence by: 
1.  **Denormalizing `assignedToId`:** For `LegalDocumentSummary` and `Asset` subcollections, the `caseAssignedToId` (the `uid` of the admin assigned to the parent `Case`) is copied directly into each document. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check the parent `Case` document, thereby enabling atomic operations (transactions/batches) and significantly simplifying rule debugging.
2.  **Direct `assignedToId` for top-level entities:** `Consultation` and `Case` documents themselves contain an `assignedToId` field, linking them directly to the managing administrator's Firebase UID. This allows for direct authorization checks within the document itself.
3.  **Dedicated Admin Role Collection:** Administrative privileges are managed via a top-level collection `/admins/{userId}`. The mere existence of a document at `/admins/$(request.auth.uid)` grants global admin access, promoting 'Existence over Content' and avoiding complex permission structures within user documents.

**Querying for Authorization Purposes (QAPs):**
This structure directly supports secure `list` operations without relying on rules as filters, which is a key QAP:
1.  **Admins Listing All:** Global administrators (whose UIDs exist in `/admins`) can securely `list` all documents in `/consultations` and `/cases` because their 'admin' status is checked once at the top level, and the collections are designed for administrative access.
2.  **Assigned Admin Listing:** An administrator can `list` only the `Consultations` or `Cases` they are `assignedTo` by querying the `assignedToId` field directly (`collection.where('assignedToId', '==', request.auth.uid)`). This is efficient and secure because the `assignedToId` is denormalized within each document.
3.  **Subcollection Listing:** For `LegalDocumentSummary` and `Asset` documents, `list` operations within a specific `caseId` are secure. Access is granted if the `request.auth.uid` matches the denormalized `caseAssignedToId` present in every document within that subcollection, or if the user is a global admin.

**Structural Segregation:**
All documents within a given collection or subcollection path share the same security requirements. For example, all `Consultation` documents are accessible by administrators, and all `LegalDocumentSummary` documents within a `Case` have security tied to that specific case's `assignedToId`. This greatly simplifies Firestore security rules, moving complexity from logic to structure.
